question: Of 75 patients in a follow-up study, 20 showed spontaneous improvement within a month. Calculate the 1-month incidence proportion of improvement. Include a 95% confidence interval for the proportion.
answer:
Incidence $\hat{p}= rac{20}{75}=0.2667$ (about $27 \%$ )
Confidence interval for $p$ by the plus-four method:

$$
 egin{aligned}
&  ar{n}=79 \
&  ar{x}=22 \
&  ar{p}=0.2785 \
& S E_{ ar{p}}=\sqrt{ rac{0.2785)(0.7215)}{79}}=0.0504
\end{aligned}
$$

$95 \%$ confidence interval for $p=0.2785 \pm(1.96)(0.0504)=0.2785 \pm 0.0988=$ 0.1797 to 0.3773 or about $18 \%$ to $38 \%$.
